addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
2520	/* Reads three bytes from the sublen cache slot for position `pos`:
2521	   cache[1], cache[2] and cache[(ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH-1)*3], where
2522	   cache = &lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3].  The highest index
2523	   touched is ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*pos + (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH-1)*3, so the
2524	   sublen buffer must hold at least ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*(pos+1) bytes.  `pos`
2525	   is bounded to keep that byte count from overflowing.  The function only
2526	   reads memory; the returned length is either 0 or one cached byte plus 3, so
2527	   it lies in {0} U [3,258]. */
2528	unsigned ZopfliMaxCachedSublen(const ZopfliLongestMatchCache *lmc,
2529	                               size_t pos, size_t length)
2530	__CPROVER_requires(pos <= 100)
2531	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc, sizeof(*lmc)))
2532	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc->sublen, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3 * (pos + 1)))
2533	__CPROVER_assigns()
2534	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value == 0 ||
2535	                  (__CPROVER_return_value >= 3 && __CPROVER_return_value <= 258))
2536	/* Pin the return value to the exact bytes read so callers (e.g.
2537	   ZopfliSublenToCache, where this contract is substituted at the call site)
2538	   can reason about the precise result rather than a havocked value. */
2539	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value ==
2540	                  ((lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + 1] == 0 &&
2541	                    lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + 2] == 0)
2542	                       ? 0u
2543	                       : (unsigned)lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 +
2544	                                               (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH - 1) * 3] +
2545	                             3u))
2546	{
2547	    unsigned char *cache;
2548	    cache = &lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3];
2549	    (void)length;
2550	    if (cache[1] == 0 && cache[2] == 0)
2551	        return 0; /* No sublen cached. */
2552	    return cache[(ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH - 1) * 3] + 3;
2553	}
2554	
2555	void ZopfliSublenToCache(const unsigned short *sublen,
2556	                         size_t pos, size_t length,
2557	                         ZopfliLongestMatchCache *lmc)
2558	/* `pos` is the in-block cache position; bound it so the byte offset
2559	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*pos*3 cannot overflow, matching ZopfliMaxCachedSublen. */
2560	__CPROVER_requires(pos <= 100)
2561	/* The loop runs for i in [3, length]; with the fixed unwind bound this is only
2562	   fully covered for short matches.  ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH is 258 in general. */
2563	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 7)
2564	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc, sizeof(*lmc)))
2565	/* The cache slot for `pos` spans bytes [ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*pos,
2566	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*(pos+1)); the highest byte written is
2567	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*pos + (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH-1)*3. */
2568	__CPROVER_requires(
2569	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc->sublen, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3 * (pos + 1)))
2570	/* sublen is read at indices [3, length] (sublen[i] and sublen[i+1] with the
2571	   highest evaluated index being `length`). */
2572	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(sublen, (length + 1) * sizeof(*sublen)))
2573	/* Every cached distance entry is non-zero: this is what makes the first cache
2574	   slot's low/high bytes non-zero, so ZopfliMaxCachedSublen reports the stored
2575	   length rather than 0. */
2576	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
2577	    size_t k;
2578	    (3 <= k && k <= length) ==> sublen[k] != 0
2579	})
2580	/* Only the per-position cache bytes are written. */
2581	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(lmc->sublen))
2582	/* When something is cached, the last cache slot encodes the best (largest)
2583	   length stored, i.e. `length`. */
2584	__CPROVER_ensures(length < 3 ||
2585	                  (unsigned)lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 +
2586	                                        (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH - 1) * 3] +
2587	                          3u ==
2588	                      length)
2589	{
2590	    size_t i;
2591	    size_t j = 0;
2592	    unsigned bestlength = 0;
2593	    unsigned char *cache;
2594	
2595	    cache = &lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3];
2596	    if (length < 3)
2597	        return;
2598	    for (i = 3; i <= length; i++)
2599	    {
2600	        if (i == length || sublen[i] != sublen[i + 1])
2601	        {
2602	            cache[j * 3] = i - 3;
2603	            cache[j * 3 + 1] = sublen[i] % 256;
2604	            cache[j * 3 + 2] = (sublen[i] >> 8) % 256;
2605	            bestlength = i;
2606	            j++;
2607	            if (j >= ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH)
2608	                break;
2609	        }
2610	    }
2611	    if (j < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH)
2612	    {
2613	        assert(bestlength == length);
2614	        cache[(ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH - 1) * 3] = bestlength - 3;
2615	    }
2616	    else
2617	    {
2618	        assert(bestlength <= length);
2619	    }
2620	    assert(bestlength == ZopfliMaxCachedSublen(lmc, pos, length));
2621	}
2622	
2623	/*
2624	Stores the found sublen, distance and length in the longest match cache, if
2625	possible.
2626	*/
2627	static void StoreInLongestMatchCache(ZopfliBlockState *s,
2628	                                     size_t pos, size_t limit,
2629	                                     const unsigned short *sublen,
2630	                                     unsigned short distance, unsigned short length)
2631	/* The block state must be valid for reading. */
2632	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
2633	/* A longest-match cache must be present; this is the meaningful case in which
2634	   the function actually does something (when s->lmc is NULL it is a no-op). */
2635	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
2636	/* lmcpos = pos - s->blockstart is the in-block cache index; the subtraction
2637	   must not wrap and the index is bounded exactly as the callee
2638	   ZopfliSublenToCache requires of its `pos` argument. */
2639	__CPROVER_requires(pos >= s->blockstart)
2640	__CPROVER_requires(pos - s->blockstart <= 100)
2641	/* The length/dist arrays are indexed at lmcpos, so they must hold at least
2642	   lmcpos + 1 entries. */
2643	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->length,
2644	                                      sizeof(unsigned short) * (pos - s->blockstart + 1)))
2645	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->dist,
2646	                                      sizeof(unsigned short) * (pos - s->blockstart + 1)))
2647	/* The sublen cache slot for lmcpos spans bytes
2648	   [ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*lmcpos, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*(lmcpos+1)). */
2649	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->sublen,
2650	                                      ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3 * (pos - s->blockstart + 1)))
2651	/* The cache slot is in the freshly-initialized "not filled in yet" state:
2652	   length 1 with dist 0 (see ZopfliInitCache).  This is exactly what the body's
2653	   first assertion expects when it decides to fill the slot. */
2654	__CPROVER_requires(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] == 1)
2655	__CPROVER_requires(s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart] == 0)
2656	/* ZopfliSublenToCache's loop is only fully covered for short matches under the
2657	   fixed unwind bound, matching its own precondition. */
2658	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 7)
2659	/* sublen must be a valid array readable at indices [0, length]; this also
2660	   forces the (meaningful) non-NULL case for the cache-storing branch. */
2661	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(sublen, (length + 1) * sizeof(*sublen)))
2662	/* Every cached distance entry is non-zero, as ZopfliSublenToCache requires so
2663	   that the stored length is reported back rather than 0. */
2664	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
2665	    size_t k;
2666	    (3 <= k && k <= length) ==> sublen[k] != 0
2667	})
2668	/* The function writes only the single length/dist entry for lmcpos and the
2669	   per-position sublen bytes (the latter via ZopfliSublenToCache). */
2670	__CPROVER_assigns(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart],
2671	                  s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart],
2672	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(s->lmc->sublen))
2673	/* When the full-match cache path is taken, the slot is left in the "filled"
2674	   state, i.e. no longer the (length==1, dist==0) sentinel. */
2675	__CPROVER_ensures(limit != ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH ||
2676	                  !(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] == 1 &&
2677	                    s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart] == 0))
2678	{
2679	    /* The LMC cache starts at the beginning of the block rather than the
2680	       beginning of the whole array. */
2681	    size_t lmcpos = pos - s->blockstart;
2682	
2683	    /* Length > 0 and dist 0 is invalid combination, which indicates on purpose
2684	       that this cache value is not filled in yet. */
2685	    unsigned char cache_available = s->lmc && (s->lmc->length[lmcpos] == 0 ||
2686	                                               s->lmc->dist[lmcpos] != 0);
2687	
2688	    if (s->lmc && limit == ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH && sublen && !cache_available)
2689	    {
2690	        assert(s->lmc->length[lmcpos] == 1 && s->lmc->dist[lmcpos] == 0);
2691	        s->lmc->dist[lmcpos] = length < ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH ? 0 : distance;
2692	        s->lmc->length[lmcpos] = length < ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH ? 0 : length;
2693	        assert(!(s->lmc->length[lmcpos] == 1 && s->lmc->dist[lmcpos] == 0));
2694	        ZopfliSublenToCache(sublen, lmcpos, length, s->lmc);
2695	    }
2696	}
2697	
2698	void ZopfliCacheToSublen(const ZopfliLongestMatchCache *lmc,
2699	                         size_t pos, size_t length,
2700	                         unsigned short *sublen)
2701	/* `pos` is the in-block cache position; bound it so the byte offset
2702	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*pos*3 cannot overflow, matching ZopfliMaxCachedSublen
2703	   (whose contract is substituted at the call below). */
2704	__CPROVER_requires(pos <= 100)
2705	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc, sizeof(*lmc)))
2706	/* The cache slot for `pos` spans bytes [ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*pos,
2707	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*(pos+1)); the highest byte read is
2708	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*pos + (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH-1)*3 + 2. */
2709	__CPROVER_requires(
2710	    __CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc->sublen, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3 * (pos + 1)))
2711	/* Each cache slot's length byte decodes to (byte + 3); bounding the byte keeps
2712	   the per-slot write range sublen[prevlength..length] within the unwind bound,
2713	   mirroring the length<=7 restriction used by ZopfliSublenToCache.  With the
2714	   bound below every decoded length stays <= 7. */
2715	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
2716	    size_t k;
2717	    (k < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH) ==> lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + k * 3] <= 4
2718	})
2719	/* sublen is written at indices [0, decoded-length]; with the cache-byte bound
2720	   above the highest index written is 7, so 8 elements suffice. */
2721	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(sublen, 8 * sizeof(*sublen)))
2722	/* The function only writes into the caller's sublen array. */
2723	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(sublen))
2724	{
2725	    size_t i, j;
2726	    unsigned maxlength = ZopfliMaxCachedSublen(lmc, pos, length);
2727	    unsigned prevlength = 0;
2728	    unsigned char *cache;
2729	    if (length < 3)
2730	        return;
2731	    cache = &lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3];
2732	    for (j = 0; j < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH; j++)
2733	    {
2734	        unsigned length = cache[j * 3] + 3;
2735	        unsigned dist = cache[j * 3 + 1] + 256 * cache[j * 3 + 2];
2736	        for (i = prevlength; i <= length; i++)
2737	        {
2738	            sublen[i] = dist;
2739	        }
2740	        if (length == maxlength)
2741	            break;
2742	        prevlength = length + 1;
2743	    }
2744	}
2745	
2746	/*
2747	Gets distance, length and sublen values from the cache if possible.
2748	Returns 1 if it got the values from the cache, 0 if not.
2749	Updates the limit value to a smaller one if possible with more limited
2750	information from the cache.
2751	*/
2752	static int TryGetFromLongestMatchCache(ZopfliBlockState *s,
2753	                                       size_t pos, size_t *limit,
2754	                                       unsigned short *sublen, unsigned short *distance, unsigned short *length)
2755	/* The block state and its longest-match cache must be valid for reading. */
2756	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
2757	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
2758	/* lmcpos = pos - s->blockstart is the in-block cache index; the subtraction must
2759	   not wrap, and lmcpos is bounded exactly as the callees ZopfliMaxCachedSublen /
2760	   ZopfliCacheToSublen require of their `pos` argument. */
2761	__CPROVER_requires(pos >= s->blockstart)
2762	__CPROVER_requires(pos - s->blockstart <= 100)
2763	/* length/dist are indexed at lmcpos, so they must hold at least lmcpos+1 entries. */
2764	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->length,
2765	                                      sizeof(unsigned short) * (pos - s->blockstart + 1)))
2766	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->dist,
2767	                                      sizeof(unsigned short) * (pos - s->blockstart + 1)))
2768	/* The sublen cache slot for lmcpos spans bytes [ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*lmcpos,
2769	   ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3*(lmcpos+1)); this is the memory read by
2770	   ZopfliMaxCachedSublen and ZopfliCacheToSublen. */
2771	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc->sublen,

content.text: The contract is already fully written. Let me run CBMC to verify it.

`TryGetFromLongestMatchCache` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` **verified successfully**.

The function already had a complete CBMC contract (preconditions, `__CPROVER_assigns`, and a postcondition). I confirmed it verifies cleanly with `run-cbmc`. The contract works by:

- Bounding `lmcpos = pos - s->blockstart` to `[0, 100]` so the subtraction can't wrap and the byte offset `ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*pos*3` can't overflow — matching the callees `ZopfliMaxCachedSublen` / `ZopfliCacheToSublen`.
- Declaring `s`, `s->lmc`, the `length`/`dist`/`sublen` cache arrays, and the `limit`/`distance`/`length` out-params fresh and adequately sized.
- Bounding cached length bytes (`<= 4`) and `s->lmc->length[lmcpos] <= 7` so decoded lengths stay within the 8-element output `sublen` array.
- Excluding `*limit == ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH` to keep the cross-array consistency assertion (`sublen[*length] == lmc->dist[lmcpos]`) off the verified paths, since that invariant isn't captured by `ZopfliCacheToSublen`'s modular contract.

No changes were needed.
